metadata.title: The efficiency counter-thesis — DeepSeek, Jevons, and China's domestic AI stack (the strongest honest challenge to the AI-capex bubble case)
metadata.status: facts from press/CSIS/RAND/company; the 'unfalsifiable build-more narrative is itself bubble-evidence' framing is labeled interpretation. Steelman-then-assess, in the project's zero-trust spirit.
metadata.frame: The honest counter-argument to this project's own thesis. If algorithms get 10-50x more efficient (DeepSeek), maybe the $1.4T brute-force compute bet is unnecessary - OR (Jevons paradox) efficiency drives so much new demand the capex is justified. We hold BOTH and note the tell: the narrative is used to justify spending NO MATTER WHAT (scarce compute -> build more; cheap compute -> build more) - which is itself a bubble-narrative structure. Separately, China is routing AROUND the chip chokepoint via efficiency (DeepSeek) + a domestic brute-force stack (Huawei Ascend), undermining the export-control strategy. Overlay; excluded from the proofs. Connects to fin-microsoft-openai (the $1.4T), fin-ai-depreciation-debttrap, geopolitics-chip-chokepoint-war, macro-ai-power-grid-bottleneck.

the_deepseek_shock.event: DeepSeek R1 (late Jan 2025): frontier-comparable performance at a small fraction of the claimed training cost. Markets read it as 'compute is over-valued' - Nvidia lost ~$600B of market cap in a single day, the largest one-day loss in history. status: fact.
the_deepseek_shock.the_two_readings: BEAR (efficiency obviates brute force): if a model this good costs ~1/10-1/50 the compute, the $1.4T of OpenAI compute commitments + ~$600B/yr hyperscaler capex looks over-built -> supports the 'largest misallocation of capital in history' view (cf. the justdario thesis in fin-ai-depreciation-debttrap). BULL (Jevons paradox): cheaper intelligence -> demand explodes; Nadella tweeted 'Jevons paradox strikes again'; Goldman's 2026 forecast: at ~$0.02/kWh, AI inference is cheaper than human labor for ~23% of service jobs. status: both are fact-based arguments.

the_tell: What happened next is the diagnostic: despite DeepSeek, 2026 hyperscaler capex GREW to ~$602B (+36% YoY, CreditSights). The narrative resolved BULLISH either way - when compute was scarce you needed to build more; when DeepSeek made it cheap, Jevons said you needed to build more. A thesis that justifies the same action under opposite facts is UNFALSIFIABLE - the structural signature of a bubble narrative, not an analysis. (This does not prove over-building; it flags that 'efficiency = bullish' is being asserted, not demonstrated.) status: interpretation (labeled).

china_routes_around_the_chokepoint.the_stack: China is building an AI stack SEPARATE from Nvidia/CUDA. Huawei plans ~600,000 Ascend 910C chips in 2026 (~1.6M Ascend dies total); primary customers Alibaba, Tencent, DeepSeek (export-control + state pressure). status: fact.
china_routes_around_the_chokepoint.efficiency_vs_brute_force: Two routes around the chokepoint: (1) EFFICIENCY - DeepSeek does more with less (a Huawei-led team post-trained DeepSeek V4-Pro, a 1.6T-param model, on ~1,000 Ascend 910C); (2) BRUTE FORCE with subsidized power - the CloudMatrix 384 (384x 910C) rivals Nvidia NVL72 on inference but draws ~560kW vs ~145kW (4.1x the power for ~1.7x the compute) - inefficient, but China subsidizes energy for priority industries so it's commercially viable. status: fact.
china_routes_around_the_chokepoint.implication: The export-control strategy (geopolitics-chip-chokepoint-war) is being eroded from two sides at once - smarter algorithms need fewer chips, and a domestic (if power-hungry) stack supplies the rest. CSIS/RAND debate whether DeepSeek UNDERMINES or REINFORCES the case for controls; a US lawmaker alleged (Jan 2026) Nvidia co-designed the DeepSeek model. status: fact (the debate) + contested (the co-design claim).

honest_assessment: Where this leaves the project's bubble thesis: the CIRCULAR-FUNDING + SELF-MARKED-VALUE + DEPRECIATION proofs do NOT depend on whether compute demand is real - they are about how the build is FINANCED and ACCOUNTED FOR, which is defective regardless of end-demand. But the efficiency question DOES bear on the 'is the capex justified by real demand?' question, and here intellectual honesty requires saying: it is genuinely uncertain, the Jevons case is real, and the project should NOT claim the demand is fake. What it CAN say: (a) the unfalsifiable 'build-more-either-way' narrative is bubble-shaped; (b) efficiency makes the DEPRECIATION trap worse (today's expensive brute-force GPUs are obsoleted faster by tomorrow's efficiency); and (c) China's efficiency + domestic stack undercut the chip-chokepoint moat the bull case leans on.
